title: [lichen_util.h:141] lichen_link_strerror returns static string without bounds check
description:
What: lichen_link_strerror() is defined as inline in the header. Multiple compilation units including this header will each get their own copy of the string literals.

Why it matters: This is a code size issue, not a correctness bug. Each .c file that includes lichen_util.h will have its own copy of all the string literals ('invalid argument', 'buffer too small', etc.). For embedded systems with limited flash, this bloat adds up.

Solution: Mark the function as 'static inline' (already done) and ensure the linker merges identical strings, OR move implementation to lichen_util.c and make it a regular function.

Since it's already 'static inline' and compilers typically merge identical string literals, this is low priority.
close_reason: The function returns string literals directly from switch cases, not via array indexing. String literals are compile-time constants in .rodata - always valid and null-terminated. No bounds check is needed or possible.
